notes:
I've completed the first look at the **SIGNAL** drop microsite. 

The design leans heavily into that industrial, "broadcast" aesthetic we discussed. I've focused on:
- **High-Impact Visuals:** Using massive campaign posters and oversized stills to make it feel like a high-end editorial piece.
- **Urgent Typography:** Bold, heavy sans-serif headlines in signal orange against deep black to create immediate visual tension.
- **Technical Detailing:** Mono-spaced captions and "Unit" nomenclature to give the products a speculative, premium edge.
- **The Ticking Clock:** A prominent countdown to keep the focus on the upcoming release without the typical clutter of a store page.

How does this direction feel for the brand? We can refine the product "Specimen" sections or experiment with more motion-heavy layouts next.
prompt: Create a fashion drop microsite for a streetwear label with bold sans headlines, stacked campaign posters, release countdown cues, oversized look stills, editorial product captions, and sharp black-and-signal-orange pacing. Keep it image-led, urgent, and premium without turning into raw ecommerce.
suggestions: Add a 'Technical Specifications' section to one of the looks
Try a mobile version of this design
title: SIGNAL - Drop 004 Microsite
